summary: The Pester suite covers all six core functional requirements well: cartesian product generation, include/exclude rules, feature flags, max-parallel, fail-fast, and size validation. JSON roundtrip and a GitHub Actions-flavored output test are also present. Design is clean — proper Describe/Context/It hierarchy, independent fixtures, BeforeAll dot-source, and expressive Should assertions make it readable and maintainable. Rigor is the weak point: boundary conditions around include/base overlap (e.g., what happens when an include entry duplicates a base entry) are untested; the 'default max size of 256' test only fires one entry, leaving the upper boundary untested; there are no tests for multiple concurrent exclude rules, null/non-string dimension values, or a single-dimension matrix. The test-harness.ps1 act integration has brittle assertions (hard-coding 'Tests Passed: 18', a logically malformed failed-test check) and does not constitute a real Pester test, so it adds false confidence without strong guarantees. The workflow structure tests mentioned in requirements (check triggers, verify file paths, assert actionlint exit code) are entirely absent from the Pester suite, leaving a gap between stated requirements and tested behavior.
